issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. This removes the focus indicator entirely for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which keeps the content visible in the layout and does not hide it from screen readers as intended. This breaks the accessible hiding pattern and may cause layout issues while failing to provide the intended screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, resulting in a 28×28 px touch target. This is below the 44×44 px recommended size (WCAG 2.5.5) and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, creating a 20×20 px touch target. This is below the 24×24 px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to select.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class defines a transition on the transform property (line 293) which is triggered on :active with translateY(1px) (line 532). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary class includes transform in its transition (line 322) and applies translateY(-1px) on hover (line 686). No reduced-motion media query overrides this spatial animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transition: transform (line 485) to animate from translateY(-200%) to visible. While functional, this spatial movement lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to respect user preferences for reduced animation.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on h1-h6
issuesFound.explanation: The heading elements h1 through h6 set letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight), where --letter-spacing-tight is defined as -0.025em (negative). Negative letter-spacing reduces text readability, particularly for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which resolves to -0.025em. This negative spacing tightens text and reduces readability for button labels.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 273: 
  letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal);

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on .hero-content h1

Line 316:

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This makes keyboard focus invisible for all interactive elements that do not override this rule, blocking keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 334: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users will not see which button is focused, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 493: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (.btn)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height to 32px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes buttons difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 483: .btn { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (.btn-sm)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height to 36px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. Small buttons are hard to tap accurately on touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (Search button)
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] is explicitly sized to 28px × 28px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum and 44px recommended size. It is too small for reliable activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 676: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (Checkbox)
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] is sized to 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px WCAG minimum requirement. Users may struggle to check the box.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1381: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (Footer links)
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav links have min-height: 24px with no padding, resulting in a target size below the 44px recommended size. This degrades the experience for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1401: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class is broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Elements intended for screen readers only will be visible to sighted users, cluttering the interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 353: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 388 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 388: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast (.feature-card p)
issuesFound.explanation: The text color #aaaaaa on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of 2.3:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text. Users with low vision may not read the content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 923: .feature-card p { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast (.testimonial-card figcaption p)

issuesFound.fix: Line 1313: .testimonial-card figcaption p { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p element uses color #aaaaaa (var(--colour-text-secondary)) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 552: `.section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline with outline: none and only sets outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for button elements when navigated via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 456: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36px with vertical padding of only 6px (var(--space-1-5)), resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44px without sufficient padding to compensate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 504: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which meets the minimum size but is below the recommended 44px without padding compensation to extend the interactive area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 712: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and width: auto, which keeps the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while preserving it for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 385-395: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with no padding
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit height and width of 28px with zero padding, resulting in a touch target of only 28px which is below the recommended 44px and lacks compensating padding.
issuesFound.fix: Line 523: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit height and width of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 699: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property for the :active state animation, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion media query override to disable this spatial movement for users with motion sensitivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 455: `.btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 32px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); text-align: center; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; border: 2px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); vertical-align: middle; position: relative; isolation: isolate; }` and add after Line 459: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition for a hover lift effect, but does not provide a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 559: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-size: var(--font-size-lg); padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-8); min-height: 52px; border-radius: var(--radius-xl); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }` and add after Line 567: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove underlines with text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover, failing to provide a non-colour visual differentiator required by WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); text-decoration: underline; color: var(--colour-neutral-700); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Per WCAG 2.4.11, outline-offset alone is NOT a visible focus ring — it applies offset to a zero-width outline, rendering nothing visible. Keyboard users cannot perceive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 389-392: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. This removes the focus ring entirely for keyboard users interacting with buttons. outline-offset alone does not create a visible indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 465-467: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator uses box-shadow instead of outline on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus selector sets outline: none and relies on box-shadow for the focus ring. Per WCAG guidance, box-shadow is suppressed entirely in Windows High Contrast Mode (Forced Colors), making the focus indicator invisible for users who need it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 689-692: #search-input:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator uses box-shadow instead of outline on form inputs:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus selector sets outline: none and relies on box-shadow. This focus indicator will be invisible in High Contrast Mode, blocking access for users who rely on forced-colors settings.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1609-1611: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0. While 28px exceeds the 24px absolute minimum, it falls below the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 699-702: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px with padding: var(--space-1-5) (6px) on top/bottom. Since padding is less than 8px per side, the effective touch area may not reach the 44px recommended minimum for users with dexterity limitations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 917-919: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width: 20px and height: 20px, which is BELOW the 24px absolute minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size. This is a critical barrier for users with motor impairments who cannot reliably activate targets smaller than 24×24px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1619-1621: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer nav links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no padding specified. While 24px meets the absolute minimum, it falls below the 44px recommended size. Users with touch or motor impairments may have difficulty activating these links reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1665-1667: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto. This does NOT hide content visually — it renders normally on screen. Screen-reader-only content like skip links and section labels will be visible to sighted users, breaking the intended design.
issuesFound.fix: Line 413-422: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) with transition: transform var(--transition-base). The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 835-837 is empty and provides no override. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this spatial animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 835-837: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transition: transform var(--transition-fast) among its transitions. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this transform transition for buttons. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 875-880: Add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block: .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa. On the page background #ffffff, this produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.33:1, well below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3). Users with low vision cannot read this text reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 754-756: .section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin-top: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on background #ffffff. The 2.33:1 contrast ratio fails WCAG 1.4.3 minimum requirements. Users with moderate vision loss will struggle to read feature descriptions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1037-1039: .feature-card p { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; flex-grow: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .step-content p
issuesFound.explanation: The .step-content p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on background #ffffff. At 2.33:1 contrast, this text fails WCAG 1.4.3. Users relying on screen magnification or with reduced contrast sensitivity cannot access step instructions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1121-1123: .step-content p { font-size: var(--font-size-base); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .plan-tagline
issuesFound.explanation: The .plan-tagline selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on background #ffffff. The 2.33:1 contrast ratio is below the 4.5:1 threshold. Pricing plan taglines become illegible for users with vision impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1306-1308: .plan-tagline { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); margin: 0; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-wide); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on background #ffffff. At 2.33:1 contrast, helper text fails WCAG 1.4.3. Users cannot read form guidance, leading to input errors and frustration.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1600-1601: .input-hint { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .products-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The .products-section .section-header p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on background #ffffff. The 2.33:1 contrast ratio fails WCAG 1.4.3 minimum. Section descriptions in the products area are unreadable for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1187-1189: .products-section .section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .price-period
issuesFound.explanation: The .price-period selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on background #ffffff. At 2.33:1 contrast, pricing period text (e.g., "/month") fails WCAG 1.4.3. Users cannot distinguish pricing information accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1354-1357: .price-period { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); align-self: flex-end; margin-bottom: var(--space-1); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .testimonial-card figcaption p
issuesFound.explanation: The .testimonial-card figcaption p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on background #ffffff. The 2.33:1 contrast ratio fails WCAG 1.4.3. Testimonial author details become illegible for users with vision impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1479-1480: .testimonial-card figcaption p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .contact-section > p
issuesFound.explanation: The .contact-section > p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on background var(--colour-bg-subtle) (#f8fafc). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.2:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum. Contact section introductory text is unreadable for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1645-1647: .contact-section > p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); text-align: center; max-width: 560px; margin-inline: auto; margin-bottom: var(--space-10); padding-inline: var(--space-6); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no non-colour alternative on .feature-card a
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing the underline from links within feature cards. While hover state adds an underline (line 1072-1074), at rest these links rely on colour alone (var(--colour-brand-primary)) to distinguish them from surrounding text. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 998-1000: .feature-card a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-1); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; margin-top: auto; transition: color var(--transition-fast), gap var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on navigation hover state
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state for navigation links uses var(--colour-brand-primary) (#005fcc) text on var(--colour-info-light) (#dbeafe) background, producing a contrast ratio of approximately 4.0:1. This falls below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text, making the text difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 616: color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); or background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline with outline: none and provides only an outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus indicator for buttons when navigated via keyboard. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 410: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which renders the content fully visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to assistive technologies. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only behavior and may expose visual content that should be hidden or hide content that should be visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 368-378: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width of 28px and height of 28px with zero padding, resulting in a 28×28 pixel touch target. This falls below the 44×44px recommended size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 682: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width of 20px and height of 20px, which is below the 24×24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the control difficult to activate for users with tremors or limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1533: width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on current page navigation link
issuesFound.explanation: The current page indicator for navigation links uses var(--colour-brand-primary) (#005fcc) text on var(--colour-info-light) (#dbeafe) background with a font-weight of 600 (semibold). Since 600 is below the 700 threshold for "bold" text, this is considered normal text requiring 4.5:1 contrast. The actual ratio of approximately 4.0:1 fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum).
issuesFound.fix: Line 622: color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); or background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow in the same rule block. outline-offset alone does not create a visible ring. This removes keyboard focus indication for all elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset. This removes the focus ring for buttons when navigated via keyboard, failing WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes buttons difficult to activate on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size. Small buttons are hard to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px × 28px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum and 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is sized to 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required for pointer input.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but is below the 44px recommended size for easy activation.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility on .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually for screen readers only. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) with a transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 438 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active state uses transform: translateY(1px) to create a press effect, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this movement for users who request reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on blockquote
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.2:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card body text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.2:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links use --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on a dark background (#0f172a). The contrast ratio is approximately 4:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. Links must be distinguishable by more than color alone (e.g., underline) to meet WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social links have text-decoration: none and rely only on color change on hover. An underline or other non-color cue is required.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom text uses --colour-neutral-500 (#64748b) on a dark background (#0f172a). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.6:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the outline is set to none with only outline-offset, which renders focus completely invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode where box-shadow is also suppressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 371: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Outline-offset alone does not render a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users with no focus indication on any interactive element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 355: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 148: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset, rendering no visible focus indicator for buttons when they receive keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 445: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 409 is empty and provides no override to disable this transform transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px (24–43px range) with vertical padding of only 7.5px (space-2-5), which is below the 8px compensation threshold, potentially resulting in a touch target below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: .btn { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary button uses background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) with white text, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.49:1, which fails WCAG AA for normal text (requires 4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 428: .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); ... }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use transform: translateY in their hover transition, creating vertical motion without a reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base); } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-outline
issuesFound.explanation: The outline button uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on a transparent background (effectively white), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.49:1, failing WCAG AA.
issuesFound.fix: Line 440: .btn-outline { color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu links set min-height: 28px with vertical padding of 6px (space-2), totaling approximately 40px height, below the recommended 44px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 552: #nav-menu > li > a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px with vertical padding of only 6px (space-2), totaling approximately 40px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: .nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; padding: var(--space-2); ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets height: 28px with vertical padding of 6px (space-2), totaling approximately 40px height, below the recommended 44px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: #search-input { height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); ... }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline without replacement, leaving no visual indication when links receive keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 473: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button's focus-visible state suppresses the outline without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border, making the focused state invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 457: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" link arrow uses transform transition for horizontal movement on hover without a reduced-motion fallback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a::after { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm modifier sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 495: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets both min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, creating a touch target of only 20×20 pixels, below the 24px minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 507: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu buttons set min-height: 28px with vertical padding of 6px (space-2), totaling approximately 40px height, below the recommended 44px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 552: #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set min-height: 28px with vertical padding of 7.5px (space-2-5), totaling approximately 43px height, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 582: [id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, creating a 28×28 pixel touch target below both the 24px minimum and 44px recommended sizes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2); ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set width: 20px and height: 20px, creating a 20×20 pixel touch target below the 24px minimum size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 997: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px with no padding compensation, meeting the minimum but remaining below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1018: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2); ... }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses transform transition for its slide animation without a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the motion for users who require reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transform transition that creates a 3D rotation effect on hover, which lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the effect for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: Stat items use transform: translateY on hover, creating vertical motion without a reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: background-color var(--transition-base); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards use transform: translateY and scale on hover, creating spatial motion without a reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion arrow uses transform transition for rotation animation without a reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle button uses color: var(--colour-neutral-600) (#64748b) on background: var(--colour-neutral-100) (#f1f5f9), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.9:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 834: .pricing-toggle button { color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-ghost
issuesFound.explanation: The ghost button uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on transparent/white background with a contrast ratio of ~3.49:1, failing WCAG AA.
issuesFound.fix: Line 448: .btn-ghost { color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); ... }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative visual differentiator such as border-bottom, font-weight, or background highlight in the default state. This makes links indistinguishable from surrounding text except by colour, violating WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 165: a { text-decoration: underline; } OR a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements that do not have a more specific override, blocking keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative like a border or solid outline. This makes focus invisible for users relying on Windows High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector sets only outline-offset: -3px without defining an outline width, style, or color. Since the base :focus-visible rule sets outline: none, this element has no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. No box-shadow, border, or outline value is provided to replace the default focus ring, making keyboard focus invisible on buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. This removes the visible focus indicator for the mobile navigation toggle button, hindering keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown link selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. No visible focus style is provided for keyboard users navigating submenu links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and does not repeat the box-shadow defined in the :focus rule. Since :focus-visible is more specific and loaded later, the box-shadow is overridden, removing the focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search button :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. No visible focus ring is provided for the search submission button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. Keyboard users cannot see which account link is focused.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. Even with padding (6px per side), the total clickable height is 32px, which is below the recommended 44px minimum for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px. With padding (10px per side), the total size is 40px, which is below the recommended 44px minimum.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to width: 28px and height: 28px with no padding. This is significantly below the 44px minimum touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to width: 20px and height: 20px. This is below the 24px absolute minimum and 44px recommended size for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links set min-height: 24px. Without explicit padding to compensate, the effective touch target is likely below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto. This fails to hide content visually while keeping it accessible, effectively making the utility class non-functional.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transform: translateY(-200%) with a transition. The global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a high-contrast visible alternative. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed, leaving focus invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 390: Add outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; to ensure visibility in forced-colors mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline value. Keyboard users will have no visible indication of which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 375: Replace with outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; or add box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus);

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle has min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px with padding: var(--space-2) (6px), resulting in a 40px touch target, below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 615: Increase min-height and min-width to 32px, or increase padding to var(--space-3) (12px) to reach 44px total.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 85: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; or set to word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement style. Button components will not display focus rings to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: Add box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); or outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); to provide a visible indicator.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 395: Replace the entire rule with: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active selector uses transform: translateY(1px) with a transition including transform, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight) for links in their default state, causing links to be distinguishable from surrounding text only by colour.
issuesFound.fix: Line 95: Add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; or font-weight: 500; to links, or remove text-decoration: none and allow the default underline.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline on focus-visible without providing an alternative visual indicator, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the control is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: Add outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; or box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without carrying over the box-shadow from the :focus rule, leaving keyboard users without a visible focus indicator while mouse users retain the shadow.
issuesFound.fix: Line 705: Add box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); to match the :focus style, or use outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: Add outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); or box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus);

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word spacing reduces readability, especially for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary text colour #ffffff on background #5588bb has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.5:1, below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-ghost
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-ghost text colour #5588bb on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.5:1, below the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .required-marker
issuesFound.explanation: The .required-marker colour #ee9999 on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of approximately 1.75:1, far below the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p colour #aaaaaa on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, below the 3:1 requirement for large text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .logo-bar-label
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label colour #94a3b8 on background #f8fafc has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1, below the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .plan-recommended-badge
issuesFound.explanation: The .plan-recommended-badge text #ffffff on background #5588bb has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.5:1, below the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-brand > p
issuesFound.explanation: The footer text colour #94a3b8 on background #555555 has a contrast ratio of approximately 1.75:1, far below the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer nav links colour #94a3b8 on background #555555 has a contrast ratio of approximately 1.75:1, far below the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: The footer bottom text colour #64748b on background #555555 has very low contrast, below the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links have text-decoration: none and only change colour on hover. Colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer nav links have text-decoration: none and only change colour on hover. Colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social links have text-decoration: none and only change colour on hover. Colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a forced-colors-friendly alternative like a transparent outline or border. This removes focus indicators entirely for users in Windows High Contrast Mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 393-397: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid transparent; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, a negative value that reduces the space between words. This can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap at larger font sizes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 168: word-spacing: normal; (or 0)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .focus-ring-inset without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only sets a negative outline-offset without providing a visible focus ring alternative. Interactive elements using this class will have no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 401-403: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement animations. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 917: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .focus-ring-brand without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible selector sets only outline-color while outline is set to none globally. Without an outline-width or box-shadow, no focus indicator is rendered. Keyboard users cannot see when branded elements have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 405-407: .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed globally without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without an alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight change) in the base state, links are distinguished by colour alone, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 195: a { text-decoration: underline; } or provide border-bottom alternative for specific link classes

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .nav-toggle without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring. The mobile navigation toggle button will not indicate focus state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 738-741: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target potentially below recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle selector sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px with padding: var(--space-2) (6px), resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44px. While meeting the 24px minimum, the small size may still cause difficulty for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 719-720: min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons will be difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 932-933: min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target potentially below recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input selector sets explicit height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size for text inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 864: height: 44px; (or remove explicit height to allow min-height to control size)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on submenu links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without a visible replacement. Dropdown menu links will not show focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 839-843: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on search submit button without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, lacking a visible focus indicator. The search button will not indicate focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 919-922: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .btn without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no box-shadow, border, or outline replacement. Buttons throughout the interface will not show a focus ring for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 582-585: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on header logo link without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, lacking a visible replacement focus indicator. The logo link will not show focus for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 706-709: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on account navigation links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset and border-radius, providing no visible focus ring. Account navigation links will not show focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 952-956: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on feature card links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset and border-radius, lacking a visible focus indicator. Feature card links will not indicate focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1207-1211: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on FAQ buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without providing a visible replacement focus ring. FAQ accordion buttons will not show focus state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1388: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on checkbox inputs without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for checkbox controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1591-1593: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.75:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1611: color: var(--colour-neutral-300);

issuesFound.title: Touch target potentially below recommended size on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets min-height: 28px with padding-block: var(--space-2) (6px), resulting in a total height below the recommended 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1639: min-height: 32px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Checkboxes will be difficult to tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1581-1583: width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address links
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand address a selector sets color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on the footer's dark background (#555555). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.75:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1360: color: var(--colour-neutral-300);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.75:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1636: color: var(--colour-neutral-300);

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed on social links without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets text-decoration: none with no alternative non-colour visual differentiator. Social media links rely solely on colour (var(--colour-neutral-400)) to distinguish them from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1637: Add text-decoration: underline; or border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; with visible state change

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset and border-radius, but provides no visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline width. Keyboard users navigating the page will see no focus indicator on any element that uses the default focus style.
issuesFound.fix: Line 381-385: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer navigation links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset and border-radius, lacking a visible focus ring. Footer links will not indicate focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1618-1621: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target potentially below recommended size on navigation menu items
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button selectors set min-height: 28px with vertical padding of var(--space-2) (6px), resulting in a total height below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 779: min-height: 32px; (or increase padding to meet 44px total)

issuesFound.title: Touch target potentially below recommended size on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a selector sets min-height: 28px with padding var(--space-2-5) (7.5px), resulting in a total height below the recommended 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 829: min-height: 32px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target potentially below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a 28px touch target below the recommended 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 904-905: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target potentially below recommended size on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button selector sets min-height: 28px with vertical padding var(--space-2) (6px), resulting in a total height below the recommended 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1431: min-height: 32px;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active selector applies transform: translateY(1px), creating spatial movement when buttons are activated. No reduced-motion override exists for this transform.
issuesFound.fix: Line 587-589: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition, causing spatial movement. No reduced-motion override exists for this specific button variant.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1060-1064: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-1px). While there is a reduced-motion block at line 956, it only targets .hero-image img and does not cover this button transform.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1066-1071: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform in its transition, and the :hover state applies transform with perspective and rotation. The @media block at line 956 exists but is empty, providing no override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 967-971: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-slower); } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01). The existing reduced-motion override at line 1260 only targets .pricing-card:hover, not the featured variant.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1482-1485: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on blockquote text
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote selector sets color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against the default white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.14:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 235: color: var(--colour-neutral-700); (or var(--colour-text-primary))

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on navigation hover state
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a:hover selector sets color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on background-color: var(--colour-info-light) (#dbeafe). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.97:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 784: color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); (or adjust background to provide 4.5:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary selector sets color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff) on background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb). With a contrast ratio of approximately 3.54:1, this fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (12px base size).
issuesFound.fix: Line 868: background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); (darker shade) or increase font-weight/size to meet 3:1 for large text

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on outline button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-outline selector sets color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on a transparent background (assuming white). The 3.54:1 contrast ratio fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on ghost button

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger selector sets color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (#ffffff) on background-color: var(--colour-error) (#ee9999). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.03:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a selector sets color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on the card's white background. The 3.54:1 contrast ratio fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on inline code in steps
issuesFound.explanation: The .step-content code selector sets color: var(--colour-brand-secondary) (#bb88ee) on background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100) (#f1f5f9). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.37:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on active pricing toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] selector sets color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on background-color: var(--colour-bg-page) (#ffffff). The 3.54:1 contrast ratio fails the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed on header logo without alternative
