issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to hide content visually. It effectively does nothing, exposing SR-only text to sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 366: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at line 413 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 413: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override disabling this motion for this selector.
issuesFound.fix: Line 413: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform and transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at line 783 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 783: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 223: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on --colour-neutral-900 (#555555). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.65:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3 (requires 4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1448: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links use --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on --colour-neutral-900 (#555555). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.65:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1462: .social-links a { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and only change colour on hover, relying on colour alone to indicate they are links (WCAG 1.4.1).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1448: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { text-decoration: underline; }
  Line 1453: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { text-decoration: none; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have text-decoration: none and only change colour on hover, relying on colour alone to indicate they are links (WCAG 1.4.1).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1462: .social-links a { text-decoration: underline; }
  Line 1463: .social-links a:hover { text-decoration: none; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. High contrast mode users lose all focus indication because box-shadow is also suppressed in forced-colors mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 310-313: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, which does not create a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. The outline-offset property alone cannot replace a visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 299: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative word spacing reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision by crowding text together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 99: `body { ... word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Button elements lose visible focus indication, preventing keyboard users from identifying the focused button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 396: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a 28px touch target. This is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size without sufficient padding compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 569: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons require adequate touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 445: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition. This spatial movement lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 690: Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs have width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1383: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6
issuesFound.explanation: Headings use letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which evaluates to -0.025em. Negative letter spacing reduces readability, particularly for users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 121: `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { ... letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Navigation links (lines 514, 574) rely solely on colour (#5588bb) to distinguish from body text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 142: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or provide an alternative non-colour indicator such as border-bottom or font-weight change in the default state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline in focus-visible state with only outline-offset: 3px. This interactive control lacks a visible focus ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 479: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Keyboard users cannot see which link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 575: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 453: `.header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. The focus state is invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 783: `.feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible

issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons set outline: none with outline-offset: -3px. No visible focus ring is rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1341: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.explanation: Form text inputs set outline: none with outline-offset: 0. No visible focus indicator replaces the removed outline.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1390: `.form-group input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.explanation: Form select elements set outline: none with outline-offset: 0. No visible focus indicator replaces the removed outline.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-bottom > p foreground #64748b / background #1e293b ratio 3.08:1
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom text uses neutral-500 (#64748b) on neutral-900 (#1e293b) background. At font-size-xs (12px), this requires 4.5:1 but only achieves 3.08:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1427: `.footer-bottom > p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-400); ... }` (using #94a3b8 for 5.6:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Keyboard users cannot identify the focused link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1430: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. No visible focus alternative is provided.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1393: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1405: `.footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.explanation: Form textareas set outline: none with outline-offset: 0. No visible focus indicator replaces the removed outline.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1390: `.form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. No visible focus indicator is rendered.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform ...). Spatial movement animations must respect prefers-reduced-motion. The empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 319 does not override this selector.
issuesFound.fix: Line 405: Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } } or remove transform from the base transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform (perspective rotation). The empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 725 fails to disable this spatial animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 711: Add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .btn-primary foreground #ffffff / background #5588bb ratio 3.4:1
issuesFound.explanation: Primary button text (#ffffff) on brand primary background (#5588bb) has a contrast ratio of 3.4:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (font-size-base: 16px). Users with low vision cannot read the button text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: `.btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }` (using #004aaa which has 7.3:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .btn-danger foreground #ffffff / background #ee9999 ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: Danger button white text on #ee9999 background has a contrast ratio of 2.2:1, failing both 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 442: `.btn-danger { background-color: var(--colour-error-dark); border-color: var(--colour-error-dark); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }` (using #991b1b which has better contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: #nav-menu > li > a:hover foreground #5588bb / background #ffffff ratio 3.4:1
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation link hover state uses brand primary color (#5588bb) on white, providing only 3.4:1 contrast. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (font-size-sm: 14px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: `#nav-menu > li > a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); background-color: var(--colour-info-light); }` (using #004aaa for 7.3:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover foreground #5588bb / background #ffffff ratio 3.4:1
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation link hover uses brand primary (#5588bb) on white with 3.4:1 contrast, failing 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 577: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .section-header p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Section header paragraph text uses colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background. At font-size-lg (18px), this requires 3:1 contrast but only achieves 2.85:1. Users with low vision struggle to read this descriptive text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 819: `.section-header p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }` (using #475569 for 7.4:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .products-section .section-header p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Products section description uses #aaaaaa on white at 18px font size, achieving only 2.85:1 contrast (fails 3:1 for large text).
issuesFound.fix: Line 936: `.products-section .section-header p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .pricing-section .section-header p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing section description uses #aaaaaa on white at 18px font size, achieving only 2.85:1 contrast.
issuesFound.fix: Line 985: `.pricing-section .section-header p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .input-hint foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Input hint text uses #aaaaaa on white at font-size-xs (12px), requiring 4.5:1 but only achieving 2.85:1. Users cannot read helper text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1389: `.input-hint { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), :focus-visible sets outline: none. This removes the focus ring entirely for users in High Contrast Mode, blocking access.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, the function adds CSS classes to show the drawer and locks scroll, but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1678: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu opens and closes without announcing its state change to screen reader users. While the aria-expanded attribute updates (when fixed), the absence of a live region announcement means users may not realize the menu has appeared or disappeared, especially if the visual change is not accompanied by focus movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1787 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` Line 1806 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", the open function omits the corresponding true state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1780: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode toggle lacks keyboard accessibility safeguards
issuesFound.explanation: The event delegation for view mode toggles (grid/list) attaches click handlers to elements matching '[data-view-mode]'. If the HTML uses non-button elements (like divs or spans), they will not be keyboard accessible. The code sets aria-pressed assuming button semantics but does not enforce role="button", tabindex="0", or keydown handlers for non-native buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2403: Before adding the click listener, ensure keyboard support: `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(el => { if (el.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(el, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(el, 'tabindex', '0'); el.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); el.click(); } }); } });`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the function animates the panel and removes the hidden attribute, but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry that leaves screen readers unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2665: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open() does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is expected to trap focus while open (evidenced by the close function checking `_trap.isActive()`), but the open function never initializes or activates the focus trap. This allows keyboard focus to escape to the background content while the drawer is open, violating modal dialog focus management requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1689: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open() does not move focus to drawer content
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus remains on the trigger button. For a modal drawer pattern, focus should move to the drawer container or the first focusable element within it to ensure keyboard users are positioned in the new content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1690: Add `_drawer.focus({ preventScroll: true });` or focus the first link inside the drawer.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-haspopup attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button controls a menu drawer but lacks the aria-haspopup attribute to indicate it opens a popup/menu. This attribute helps screen readers understand the button's purpose before activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1720: Add `if (!_trigger.hasAttribute('aria-haspopup')) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true');` alongside the aria-expanded initialization.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element (referenced by _drawer) is given role="dialog" implicitly by the focus trap or as a navigation landmark, but it lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute to identify its purpose to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1711: Add `if (!_drawer.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_drawer.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')) setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-label', 'Mobile navigation');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This prevents screen readers from announcing the field is invalid, even though visual error styles are applied.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch where the button never announces "expanded".

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The close function sets it to "false", causing screen readers to misreport the menu state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger. The close function sets it to "false", leaving the expanded state unannounced to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer visibility not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and close functions manage visibility via CSS classes on the drawer element but do not toggle aria-hidden. When closed, the drawer content may still be accessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner appearance not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._showBanner function displays the dialog and traps focus but does not announce its appearance via a live region. Screen reader users may be confused by the sudden focus change without context.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chip removal not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips._onChipClick function removes filters but does not announce the change. Screen reader users are not informed that a filter has been cleared.

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan highlight not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.highlightPlan function visually emphasizes a plan card but does not announce the change. Screen reader users are unaware that a specific plan is being recommended or highlighted.

issuesFound.title: Viewport reveal animation not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The viewportReveal._reveal function makes content visible upon scroll but does not announce it. Users relying on screen readers may miss content that appears dynamically.

issuesFound.title: Statistic counter animation completion not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator._animate function updates the number visually but does not announce the final value in the onComplete callback. Screen readers may only announce the initial zero value.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds an error description via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This prevents screen readers from announcing the field as invalid, violating WCAG 4.1.2. The markValid() function correctly removes the attribute, but the setting is missing in markInvalid().
issuesFound.fix: Line 695: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid() function, before the addDescribedBy() call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer and body class, but fails to update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will not announce that the menu is expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", so the state becomes desynchronised.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1115: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds the open CSS class, but does not set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state. The corresponding _closeInstance() function at line 1260 correctly sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1248: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open));` in the _openInstance() function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion._onKeydown lacks Enter/Space activation for non-button triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The keydown handler handles Arrow keys, Home, and End for navigation between FAQ triggers, but does not handle Enter or Space keys. When the FAQ trigger is not a native <button> element (the code allows for divs with role="button" and tabindex="0"), users cannot activate the accordion with keyboard because the Enter/Space events are not captured and prevented.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function marks the FAQ item as open and shows the panel, but fails to set the trigger element's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This breaks the accordion pattern for screen reader users who rely on the expanded state announcement. The close() function at line 2178 correctly sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2158: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap is never created or activated
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares `_trap` variable at line 1080 but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). Consequently, when the mobile navigation opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, and when it closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button (the deactivate() method in close() at line 1145 checks for _trap which is always null). Keyboard users will lose focus context.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function within the mobileNav module (line 1653) sets the navigation drawer to visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close() function (line 1683) correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed when the menu opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1653: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function within the dropdownMenu module (line 1830) reveals the dropdown panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The _closeInstance() function (line 1849) correctly sets it to "false", leaving assistive technology unaware of the open state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function within the faqAccordion module (line 3430) expands the answer panel but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close() function (line 3463) correctly sets aria-expanded="false", resulting in inconsistent ARIA state reporting for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3430: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function (line 1385) associates an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This prevents screen readers from explicitly announcing the field as invalid, affecting newsletterForm, contactForm, and multiStepWizard validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Marks a form field as invalid by linking the error message via aria-describedby, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains an invalid value.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing aria-expanded and focus return
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Additionally, it does not return keyboard focus to the trigger button that opened the menu, violating WCAG focus management requirements for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add before "emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close'...": setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, the new result count is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that the filter action completed or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add after "_syncPagination(totalMatches);": liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Expands the FAQ answer panel and animates the height, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This creates an inconsistency with the close function which sets it to "false", leaving screen readers unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add after "item.open = true;": setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is changed between monthly and annual, the price change is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that the prices have updated or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add after "_updateSavingsMessage(period);": liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" on page load. The ARIA state does not reflect the initial closed state of the navigation drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add after "_initialised = true;": setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: Clears the error state and removes the aria-describedby reference, but fails to reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. The field remains marked as invalid for screen readers even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add at end of function: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class and removes hidden attributes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1345: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function removes the open class and adds hidden attributes but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1375: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init function does not initialise aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function sets up event listeners but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger button. The initial state of the menu is not communicated to assistive technologies on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1465: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Users relying on screen readers will not know the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1570: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function adds the hidden attribute to the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Users relying on screen readers will not know the dropdown is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1595: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel and removes the hidden attribute but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The expanded state is not reflected in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2685: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2340: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid function does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference but fails to remove the aria-invalid attribute. The field may continue to be announced as invalid even after the error is resolved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2360: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters function does not announce count changes
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the product count badge and visibility of cards but does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed how many products match the applied filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1975: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod function does not announce price changes
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates all plan card prices based on the billing period but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed that prices have switched between monthly and annual billing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2560: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1795: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function expands a FAQ item's panel and sets item.open = true, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger), preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3119: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function links error messages via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field, so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2653: Add `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, the function does not activate a focus trap (the _trap variable is declared but never assigned), allowing keyboard focus to escape to background elements while the modal-like menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1775: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` after opening the drawer and before emitting the open event.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes error descriptions from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", potentially leaving stale invalid state on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2670: Add `field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);` or `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` after the describedby cleanup logic.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The init function sets up the mobile navigation but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the page loads with the menu in a closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1845: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after the `_initialised = true;` line and before attaching event listeners.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the mobile navigation, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, leaving keyboard users with lost focus context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1805: Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` immediately before the `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...)` call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close function emits a custom event but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the liveRegion utility, leaving users unaware that the navigation has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1806: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` after unlocking scroll and before emitting the close event.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu updates the visual state but does not announce the expanded state or menu availability to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2020: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');` and before the requestAnimationFrame call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu removes the panel from view but does not announce the collapsed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2038: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');` before the `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...)` call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding a FAQ item reveals content but does not announce the question text or expanded state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3125: Add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' answer expanded');` after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Collapsing a FAQ item hides content but does not announce the collapsed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3148: Add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' answer collapsed');` before the `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });` call.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Applying filters updates the product grid and count badge visually but does not announce the result count or filter change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3478: Add `liveRegion.announce('${totalMatches} products found');` immediately after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` inside the filterAnimation callback.

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are validated, the code updates visual styling and error messages but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields or clear it on valid fields. Screen readers cannot programmatically identify which fields have validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1: In the `else` block (invalid case), before or alongside `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`, add: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`
  Line ~2: In the `if` block (valid case), before or alongside `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`, add: `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the code removes CSS classes but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element. The visually hidden content remains in the accessibility tree and could be reached by screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1: In `close()` function, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` alongside the `removeClass(_drawer, ...)` call.
  Line ~2: In `open()` function, add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` or `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` alongside the `addClass(_drawer, ...)` call.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, no announcement is made to screen readers via the live region. Users are not informed that content has appeared or disappeared, and focus does not automatically move to the new content, leaving screen reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1: In `open()` function, add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : \`\${item.question} expanded\`);`
  Line ~2: In `close()` function, add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : \`\${item.question} collapsed\`);`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu functions show and hide the panel via CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but they fail to update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button to indicate the expanded or collapsed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1 (in `_openInstance()`): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line ~2 (in `_closeInstance()`): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle between active and inactive states to show which filter is applied, but they do not update the `aria-pressed` attribute to indicate the pressed or selected state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1: In `_syncTabAriaStates()`, inside the `forEach` loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus management on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not programmatically moved to the drawer or its first focusable element. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button and must manually navigate into the drawer, which may be difficult to discover.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing periods, the prices update visually and the toggle state changes, but no announcement is made to screen readers about the price change or the new billing period selection.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer and body classes but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users are not informed of the menu's state change via standard ARIA state, relying solely on the live region announcement which is less robust for widget state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1716: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 1743: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance and _closeInstance functions manage the hidden attribute and CSS classes for the panel but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element (inst.trigger). This breaks the disclosure widget pattern, preventing assistive technologies from conveying the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1888: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 1904: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance and _closeInstance functions change visible content (showing/hiding the panel) but do not write to an aria-live region. Since aria-expanded is also missing (see Issue 2), screen reader users receive no announcement that the dropdown state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1888: liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');
  Line 1904: liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion module's open function sets the panel to visible and updates internal state but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where the open action is not reflected in ARIA.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2907: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to identify fields with validation errors immediately upon focus or status change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1537: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error reference from aria-describedby when a field becomes valid but does not remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute. If aria-invalid was set previously, it remains "true", incorrectly signalling an error state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1550: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle uses aria-checked on native button
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's _syncToggleState function applies aria-checked to the toggle element (_toggleEl). If this element is a native <button> (which does not receive role="switch" in init), aria-checked is invalid; native toggle buttons should use aria-pressed instead.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2636: setAttr(_toggleEl, _toggleEl.tagName === 'BUTTON' ? 'aria-pressed' : 'aria-checked', String(isYear));

issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab button for "All tools" visually indicates an active state when selected but lacks aria-pressed to communicate this state to assistive technology. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Scanning" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab button for "Scanning" visually indicates an active state when selected but lacks aria-pressed to communicate this state to assistive technology. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Design" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab button for "Design" visually indicates an active state when selected but lacks aria-pressed to communicate this state to assistive technology. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button in the NavDropdown component uses aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to assistive technology. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the submenu is currently expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button has aria-controls linking to the answer panel but is missing the aria-expanded attribute to indicate whether the panel is currently open or closed. This prevents screen reader users from understanding the state of the disclosure widget.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component receives an `invalid` prop to style the field when in an error state but does not map this to the `aria-invalid` attribute. This prevents screen readers from announcing the invalid state to users when form validation fails.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Testing" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab button for "Testing" visually indicates an active state when selected but lacks aria-pressed to communicate this state to assistive technology. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Reporting" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab button for "Reporting" visually indicates an active state when selected but lacks aria-pressed to communicate this state to assistive technology. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle "Monthly" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button for "Monthly" visually indicates selection but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to assistive technology. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which billing period is currently selected.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle "Annual" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button for "Annual" visually indicates selection but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to assistive technology. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which billing period is currently selected.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle button missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons in FilterTabs act as a toggle group to show/hide product categories, but they do not communicate their active/inactive state to assistive technology. issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The video play/pause button toggles a binary state but relies solely on a dynamic aria-label to convey state. It lacks aria-pressed, which is the standard mechanism for toggle buttons, causing inconsistent screen reader announcements.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email error message broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input field references an error message ID via aria-describedby when invalid, but the FormGroup component is not provided with the corresponding errorId prop. Consequently, the error message element is never rendered in the DOM, creating a broken ARIA reference that prevents screen readers from announcing validation errors.

    label="Email address"

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The button accepts an isLoading state and shows a spinner, but does not communicate the busy state to assistive technology via aria-busy. Screen reader users may not be informed that the button is currently processing an action.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-busy attribute to the button: `<button aria-busy={isLoading} ... >`

issuesFound.title: StarRating container missing role="img"
issuesFound.explanation: The star rating widget uses aria-label to convey the rating value but lacks role="img", so screen readers may not treat it as a single image representation. This causes the rating to be announced inconsistently or not recognized as a graphical element.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add role="img" to the outer span: `<span role="img" aria-label={...}>`

issuesFound.title: StarRating individual star icons missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters (★/☆) are decorative elements within the composite rating image but are not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce each star individually (e.g., "star star star star star"), creating verbose and confusing output.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden to the star spans: `{filled ? '★' : '☆'}`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon (▲/▼) is a decorative visual indicator of the dropdown state but is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce the arrow character, which is redundant to the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden to the span: `...`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause icon (⏸/▶) is a decorative visual element accompanying the button's accessible label, but it is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce the character, which is redundant.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel pause button icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The pause/play icon (▶/⏸) is a decorative visual element accompanying the button's accessible label, but it is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce the character, which is redundant.

issuesFound.title: Button spinner element missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner is a decorative visual element indicating a loading state, but it is not hidden from assistive technology. The button already provides aria-busy and visually hidden text, so the spinner element itself should be hidden to prevent redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden to the spinner span: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="btn-spinner" />`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div used as interactive element without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the navigation but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard-only users cannot focus or activate this element to close the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add role, tabIndex, and keyboard handler: `<div role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close navigation" onKeyDown={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ' ? onClose() : null} onClick={onClose} />`

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div used as interactive element without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay div has an onClick handler to close the modal but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard-only users cannot focus or activate this element to close the modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add role, tabIndex, and keyboard handler: `<div role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close dialog" onKeyDown={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ' ? onClose() : null} onClick={onClose} aria-modal="true">`
